What is the complete De-Energizing and Re-Energizing procedure for Electrical Energy Source Isolation (Sections 5.7.1 and 5.7.2), and what is the policy for LOTO permit retention (Section 5.7.5)? A5.
Today
3:12 PM
AI ANSWER
SOURCES (1)

AI Thoughts

5.7.1 De-Energizing
Stop the equipment; Shift Engineer prepares the permit in Infor, which auto-triggers to Electrical.
System generates tags A, B, and C; waterproof tags are printed.
Authorized Electrical Engineer/Shift Technician confirms equipment is not running, signs tags A/B/C, and isolates as per electrical SOP/SMP.
Electrical installs the HASP and locking arrangement on the de-energized feeder.
After isolation/locking, display Danger Tag A on the main switch/breaker, enter key details in the Infor permit, verify tag number appears, and mark de-energization done.
Permit returns to Operations for printing.
Electrical hands tags B and C with the key to Operations and signs the electrical isolation section of the permit.
Operations attaches tag B to the permit and places tag C in the LCS panel after try-out.
After de-energization is confirmed, Operations issues the work permit and records the Electrical Lockout Permit number, Danger Tag number, and lock number on the work permit; then hands the work permit plus Danger Tag C to the permit receiver/executor.
During the job, the executor keeps the hot/cold work permit with Danger Tag C stapled to it and the padlock key; the original Electrical Lockout Permit stays with Operations in the concerned control room. [1]
5.7.2 Re-Energizing
After work is complete, the executing agency returns the work permit with Danger Tag B to Operations.
On instruction from the Chief Operator/Engineer, the field operator checks job completion, material removal, housekeeping, guard refitting, etc., then removes Local Tag A and hands it to the Chief Operator/Engineer.
At this stage, the job is treated as complete and the maintained electrical system is ready for energizing.
After field confirmation, the Chief Operator/Engineer signs Section C of the Electrical Lockout Permit and sends the permit with Danger Tag C to Electrical.
The executing agency/person removes the lock from the HASP on the isolated feeder in the presence of the Authorized Isolator (Electrical Engineer/Shift Technician).
Electrical confirms all executing agencies have removed their locks and no one is still working, then removes the HASP and energizes the feeder as per electrical SOP/SMP.
After energizing and completing Section D, Electrical returns the original and duplicate Electrical Lockout Permit with Danger Tags B and C to Operations. [2]
5.7.5 LOTO permit retention
Closed LOTO permits are kept for 1 month before destruction.
If there is any incident, the permit must be preserved and sent to the Safety Department for records and statutory reference. [3]
